revision.text:
'''震'''（しん）は[[八卦]]の一つ。卦の形は[[画像:Shin.png]]であり、初[[爻]]・第2爻は[[陰陽思想|陰]]、第3爻は陽で構成される。または[[六十四卦]]の一つであり、震為雷。震上震下で構成される。
== 卦象 == [[雷]]・動・[[龍]]・[[足]]・長男などを象徴する。[[方位]]としては[[東]]を示す。
[[Category:易|しん]]

'''震'''（しん）は[[八卦]]の一つ。卦の形は[[画像:Shin.png]]であり、初[[爻]]は[[陰陽思想|陽]]、第2爻・第3爻は陰で構成される。または[[六十四卦]]の一つであり、震為雷。震上震下で構成される。
== 卦象 == [[雷]]・動・[[龍]]・[[足]]・長男などを象徴する。[[方位]]としては[[東]]を示す。
[[Category:易|しん]]

'''震'''（しん）は[[八卦]]の一つ。卦の形は[[画像:Shin.png]]であり、初[[爻]]は[[陰陽思想|陽]]、第2爻・第3爻は陰で構成される。または[[六十四卦]]の一つであり、震為雷。震上震下で構成される。
== 卦象 == [[雷]]・動・[[龍]]・[[足]]・長男などを象徴する。[[方位]]としては[[東]]を示す。
[[Category:易|しん]]
[[fr:Shin (Yi Jing)]] [[nl:I Ching trigram donder]]

{{八卦}} '''震'''（しん）は[[八卦]]の一つ。卦の形は[[画像:Shin.png]]であり、初[[爻]]は[[陰陽思想|陽]]、第2爻・第3爻は陰で構成される。または[[六十四卦]]の一つであり、震為雷。震下震上で構成される。
== 卦象 == [[雷]]・動・[[龍]]・[[足]]・長男などを象徴する。[[方位]]としては[[東]]（[[地支]]では[[卯]]）を示す。
[[納甲]]では[[庚]]、[[五行]]の[[金]]、[[五方]]の[[西]]が当てられる。
== 先天図 == 伏羲先天八卦における次序は四であり、方位は四隅卦の一つで[[北東]]に配される。陰陽消息は一陽が生じたところである。
[[Category:易|しん]]
[[fr:Shin (Yi Jing)]] [[nl:I Ching trigram donder]]

{{八卦}} '''震'''（しん）は[[八卦]]の一つ。卦の形は{{resize|125%|'''☳'''}}であり、初[[爻]]は[[陽]]、第2爻・第3爻は陰で構成される。または[[六十四卦]]の一つであり、[[周易下経三十四卦の一覧#震|震為雷]]。震下震上で構成される。
== 卦象 == 原義は最も下(創め)に一陽が生じて、限りなく前進する様子。ここから始動の時・雷鳴（[[雷]]の音）・[[龍]]・[[足]]・長男・若い男性などを象徴する。[[方位]]としては[[東]]を示す<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」国立国会図書館 2021年12月16日閲覧</ref>（[[地支]]では[[卯]]と一致）。現代社会では電信電話・高速通信など。時に嘘つきにもなる。肉体では聴覚・[[肝臓]]・[[代謝]]など。
[[納甲]]では[[庚]]、[[五行思想|五行]]の[[金]]、[[五方]]の[[西]]が当てられる。
== 先天図 == 伏羲先天八卦における次序は四であり、方位は四隅卦の一つで[[北東]]に配される。陰陽消息は一陽が生じたところである。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:しん}} [[Category:易]]

revision.comment: 曖昧さ回避ページ[[東]]へのリンクを解消、リンクを除去([[User:ネイ/DisamAssist|DisamAssist]]使用)
revision.text:
{{八卦}} '''震'''（しん）は[[八卦]]の一つ。卦の形は{{resize|125%|'''☳'''}}であり、初[[爻]]は[[陽]]、第2爻・第3爻は陰で構成される。または[[六十四卦]]の一つであり、[[周易下経三十四卦の一覧#震|震為雷]]。震下震上で構成される。
== 卦象 == 原義は最も下(創め)に一陽が生じて、限りなく前進する様子。ここから始動の時・雷鳴（[[雷]]の音）・[[龍]]・[[足]]・長男・若い男性などを象徴する。[[方位]]としては東を示す<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」国立国会図書館 2021年12月16日閲覧</ref>（[[地支]]では[[卯]]と一致）。現代社会では電信電話・高速通信など。時に嘘つきにもなる。肉体では聴覚・[[肝臓]]・[[代謝]]など。
[[納甲]]では[[庚]]、[[五行思想|五行]]の[[金]]、[[五方]]の[[西]]が当てられる。
== 先天図 == 伏羲先天八卦における次序は四であり、方位は四隅卦の一つで[[北東]]に配される。陰陽消息は一陽が生じたところである。

{{DEFAULTSORT:しん}} [[Category:易]]

The term "Nine S..."
revision.text:
{{otheruses|Nine Stars of Divination|Nine Stars in Indian Astronomy|Nine Luminaries}} {{Cite source|date=December 2018}} {{color}} '''Nine Stars''' ({{ill|Nine Stars|ja|九星}}, Kyūsei) is a form of [[folk belief]] originating from ancient [[China]], consisting of nine stars: '''First White''', '''Second Black''', '''Third Blue''', '''Fourth Green''', '''Fifth Yellow''', '''Sixth White''', '''Seventh Red''', '''Eighth White''', and '''Ninth Purple'''.
The term "Nine Stars" can refer to several systems, and the one described here is specifically referred to as the "Purple White Nine Stars" or "Seven Colored Stars."
== Origin == The Nine Stars system originates from the following {{ill|magic square|ja|魔方陣}}:
{| class="wikitable" |- |Four||Nine||Two |- |Three||Five||Seven |- |Eight||One||Six |}
In this magic square, the sum of any row, column, or diagonal equals 15. It uses the numbers 1 through 9 exactly once in a 3×3 grid. This configuration, called the '''Later Heaven Arrangement''' ({{ill|Later Heaven Arrangement|ja|後天定位盤}}), is the only one of its kind, excluding rotations and reflections. These numbers are paired with seven colors (white, black, blue, green, yellow, red, and purple), the {{ill|five elements|ja|五行思想}} ({{ill|wood|ja|木}}, {{ill|fire|ja|火}}, {{ill|earth|ja|土}}, {{ill|metal|ja|金}}, and {{ill|water|ja|水}}), the {{ill|ten heavenly stems|ja|十干}}, the {{ill|twelve earthly branches|ja|十二支}}, and the {{ill|eight trigrams|ja|八卦}} of {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}. When these numbers shift positions, interpretations are added to form the "Nine Stars."
According to legend, {{ill|Yu the Great|ja|禹}}, founder of the {{ill|Xia Dynasty|ja|夏王朝}}, conceived this magic square when he encountered a sacred turtle emerging from the {{ill|Luo River|ja|洛水}}. This magic square is called the "Writing of the Luo" ({{ill|Luo Writing|ja|洛書}}), one of the {{ill|River Chart and Luo Writing|ja|河図洛書}}.
== The Nine Stars == In Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, the following table associates the Nine Stars with the {{ill|five elements|ja|五行思想}}, {{ill|directions|ja|方位}}, {{ill|eight trigrams|ja|八卦}}, and colors, forming the '''Nine Stars Chart''' used to predict {{ill|fortune|ja|運勢}} and the {{ill|auspiciousness|ja|吉凶}} of directions based on a person's birth year.
{| class="wikitable" |- ! Star ! Reading ! Five Element ! Direction ! Eight Trigram ! Color |- ! {{ill|First White Water Star|ja|一白水星}} | Ippaku Suisei | Water | {{ill|North|ja|北}} | {{ill|Kan|ja|坎}} | style="background:white;"| White |- ! {{ill|Second Black Earth Star|ja|二黒土星}} | Jikoku Dosei | Earth | Southwest | {{ill|Kun|ja|坤}} | style="background:black;color:white;"| Black |- ! {{ill|Third Blue Wood Star|ja|三碧木星}} | Sanpeki Mokusei | Wood | {{ill|East|ja|東}} | {{ill|Zhen|ja|震}} | style="background:blue;color:white;"| Blue |- ! {{ill|Fourth Green Wood Star|ja|四緑木星}} | Shiroku Mokusei | Wood | Southeast | {{ill|Xun|ja|巽}} | style="background:green;color:white;"| Green |- ! {{ill|Fifth Yellow Earth Star|ja|五黄土星}} | Goō Dosei | Earth | Center | {{ill|Tai Chi|ja|太極}} | style="background:yellow;"| Yellow |- ! {{ill|Sixth White Metal Star|ja|六白金星}} | Roppaku Kinsei | Metal | Northwest | {{ill|Qian|ja|乾}} | style="background:white;"| White |- ! {{ill|Seventh Red Metal Star|ja|七赤金星}} | Shichiseki Kinsei | Metal | {{ill|West|ja|西}} | {{ill|Dui|ja|兌}} | style="background:red;color:white;"| Red |- ! {{ill|Eighth White Earth Star|ja|八白土星}} | Happaku Dosei | Earth | Northeast | {{ill|Gen|ja|艮}} | style="background:white;"| White |- ! {{ill|Ninth Purple Fire Star|ja|九紫火星}} | Kyūshi Kasei | Fire | {{ill|South|ja|南}} | {{ill|Li|ja|離}} | style="background:purple;color:white;"| Purple |}
Despite their names, the Nine Stars are not related to celestial objects like the planets in the {{ill|Solar System|ja|太陽系}}.
== Relationships Among the Stars == The relationships among the Nine Stars follow the {{ill|five elements theory|ja|五行思想}}:
{| class="wikitable" |- ! Star ! Beneficial (Compatible) ! Neutral (Compatible) ! Harmful (Incompatible) |- ! First White Water Star | Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star, Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star | First White Water Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star |- ! Second Black Earth Star | Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | First White Water Star, Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star |- ! Third Blue Wood Star | First White Water Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star | Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star, Eighth White Earth Star |- ! Fourth Green Wood Star | First White Water Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star | Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star, Eighth White Earth Star |- ! Fifth Yellow Earth Star | Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | First White Water Star, Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star |- ! Sixth White Metal Star | First White Water Star, Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star | Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star |- ! Seventh Red Metal Star | First White Water Star, Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star | Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star |- ! Eighth White Earth Star | Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | First White Water Star, Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star |- ! Ninth Purple Fire Star | Second Black Earth Star, Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | Ninth Purple Fire Star | First White Water Star, Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star |}
These relationships are used for predicting {{ill|compatibility|ja|相性}}, {{ill|fortune|ja|運勢}}, and {{ill|auspicious directions|ja|吉方位}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|I Ching|ja|易}} * {{ill|Takashima Divination|ja|高島易断}} * {{ill|Feng Shui|ja|風水}} * {{ill|Ki Astrology|ja|気学}} * {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements Theory|ja|陰陽五行説}} * {{ill|Calendar Annotations|ja|暦注}}

When these numbers shift positions, interpretations are added to form the "Nine Stars."
According to legend, {{ill|Yu the Great|ja|禹}}, founder of the {{ill|Xia Dynasty|ja|夏王朝}}, conceived this magic square when he encountered a sacred turtle emerging from the {{ill|Luo River|ja|洛水}}. This magic square is called the "Writing of the Luo" ({{ill|Luo Writing|ja|洛書}}), one of the {{ill|River Chart and Luo Writing|ja|河図洛書}}.

Color |- ! {{ill|First White Water Star|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:一白水星]] | Ippaku Suisei | Water | {{ill|North|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:北]] | {{ill|Kan|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:坎]] | style="background:white;"| White |- ! {{ill|Second Black Earth Star|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:二黒土星]] | Jikoku Dosei | Earth | Southwest | {{ill|Kun|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:坤]] | style="background:black;color:white;"| Black |- ! {{ill|Third Blue Wood Star|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:三碧木星]] | Sanpeki Mokusei | Wood | {{ill|East|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:東]] | | style="background:blue;color:white;"| Blue |- ! {{ill|Fourth Green Wood Star|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:四緑木星]] | Shiroku Mokusei | Wood | Southeast | | style="background:green;color:white;"| Green |- ! {{ill|Fifth Yellow Earth Star|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:五黄土星]] | Goō Dosei | Earth | Center | | style="background:yellow;"| Yellow |- ! {{ill|Sixth White Metal Star|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:六白金星]] | Roppaku Kinsei | Metal | Northwest | | style="background:white;"| White |- ! {{ill|Seventh Red Metal Star|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:七赤金星]] | Shichiseki Kinsei | Metal | {{ill|West|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:西]] | | style="background:red;color:white;"| Red |- ! {{ill|Eighth White Earth Star|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:八白土星]] | Happaku Dosei | Earth | Northeast | | style="background:white;"| White |- ! {{ill|Ninth Purple Fire Star|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:九紫火星]] | Kyūshi Kasei | Fire | {{ill|South|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:南]] | | style="background:purple;color:white;"| Purple |}

Eighth White Earth Star | Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | First White Water Star, Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star |- ! Ninth Purple Fire Star | Second Black Earth Star, Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | Ninth Purple Fire Star | First White Water Star, Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star |}
These relationships are used for predicting {{ill|compatibility|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:相性]], {{ill|fortune|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:運勢]], and {{ill|auspicious directions|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:吉方位]].

<nowiki>{{otheruses|Nine Stars of Divination|Nine Stars in Indian Astronomy|Nine Luminaries]] {{Cite source|date=December 2018]]</nowiki> {| class="wikitable" |- | {{ill|Zhen|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:震]] |- | {{ill|Xun|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:巽]] |- | {{ill|Tai Chi|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:太極]] |- | {{ill|Qian|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:乾]] |- | {{ill|Dui|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:兌]] |- | {{ill|Gen|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:艮]] |- | {{ill|Li|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:離]] |}{{color]] '''Nine Stars''' ({{ill|Nine Stars|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:九星]], Kyūsei) is a form of [[folk belief]] originating from ancient [[China]], consisting of nine stars: '''First White''', '''Second Black''', '''Third Blue''', '''Fourth Green''', '''Fifth Yellow''', '''Sixth White''', '''Seventh Red''', '''Eighth White''', and '''Ninth Purple'''.
The term "Nine Stars" can refer to several systems, and the one described here is specifically referred to as the "Purple White Nine Stars" or "Seven Colored Stars."
== Origin == The Nine Stars system originates from the following {{ill|magic square|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:魔方陣]]:
{| class="wikitable" |- |Four||Nine||Two |- |Three||Five||Seven |- |Eight||One||Six |}
In this magic square, the sum of any row, column, or diagonal equals 15. It uses the numbers 1 through 9 exactly once in a 3×3 grid. This configuration, called the '''Later Heaven Arrangement''' ({{ill|Later Heaven Arrangement|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:後天定位盤]]), is the only one of its kind, excluding rotations and reflections. These numbers are paired with seven colors (white, black, blue, green, yellow, red, and purple), the {{ill|five elements|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:五行思想]] ({{ill|wood|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:木]], {{ill|fire|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:火]], {{ill|earth|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:土]], {{ill|metal|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:金]], and {{ill|water|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:水]]), the {{ill|ten heavenly stems|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:十干]], the {{ill|twelve earthly branches|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:十二支]], and the {{ill|eight trigrams|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:八卦]] of {{ill|I Ching|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:易経]]. When these numbers shift positions, interpretations are added to form the "Nine Stars."
According to legend, {{ill|Yu the Great|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:禹]], founder of the {{ill|Xia Dynasty|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:夏王朝]], conceived this magic square when he encountered a sacred turtle emerging from the {{ill|Luo River|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:洛水]]. This magic square is called the "Writing of the Luo" ({{ill|Luo Writing|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:洛書]]), one of the {{ill|River Chart and Luo Writing|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:河図洛書]].
== The Nine Stars == In Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:陰陽道]], the following table associates the Nine Stars with the {{ill|five elements|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:五行思想]], {{ill|directions|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:方位]], {{ill|eight trigrams|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:八卦]], and colors, forming the '''Nine Stars Chart''' used to predict {{ill|fortune|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:運勢]] and the {{ill|auspiciousness|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:吉凶]] of directions based on a person's birth year.
{| class="wikitable" |- ! Star ! Reading ! Five Element ! Direction ! Eight Trigram ! Color |- ! {{ill|First White Water Star|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:一白水星]] | Ippaku Suisei | Water | {{ill|North|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:北]] | {{ill|Kan|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:坎]] | style="background:white;"| White |- ! {{ill|Second Black Earth Star|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:二黒土星]] | Jikoku Dosei | Earth | Southwest | {{ill|Kun|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:坤]] | style="background:black;color:white;"| Black |- ! {{ill|Third Blue Wood Star|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:三碧木星]] | Sanpeki Mokusei | Wood | {{ill|East|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:東]] | | style="background:blue;color:white;"| Blue |- ! {{ill|Fourth Green Wood Star|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:四緑木星]] | Shiroku Mokusei | Wood | Southeast | | style="background:green;color:white;"| Green |- ! {{ill|Fifth Yellow Earth Star|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:五黄土星]] | Goō Dosei | Earth | Center | | style="background:yellow;"| Yellow |- ! {{ill|Sixth White Metal Star|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:六白金星]] | Roppaku Kinsei | Metal | Northwest | | style="background:white;"| White |- ! {{ill|Seventh Red Metal Star|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:七赤金星]] | Shichiseki Kinsei | Metal | {{ill|West|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:西]] | | style="background:red;color:white;"| Red |- ! {{ill|Eighth White Earth Star|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:八白土星]] | Happaku Dosei | Earth | Northeast | | style="background:white;"| White |- ! {{ill|Ninth Purple Fire Star|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:九紫火星]] | Kyūshi Kasei | Fire | {{ill|South|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:南]] | | style="background:purple;color:white;"| Purple |}
Despite their names, the Nine Stars are not related to celestial objects like the planets in the {{ill|Solar System|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:太陽系]].
== Relationships Among the Stars == The relationships among the Nine Stars follow the {{ill|five elements theory|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:五行思想]]:
{| class="wikitable" |- ! Star ! Beneficial (Compatible) ! Neutral (Compatible) ! Harmful (Incompatible) |- ! First White Water Star | Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star, Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star | First White Water Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star |- ! Second Black Earth Star | Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | First White Water Star, Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star |- ! Third Blue Wood Star | First White Water Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star | Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star, Eighth White Earth Star |- ! Fourth Green Wood Star | First White Water Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star | Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star, Eighth White Earth Star |- ! Fifth Yellow Earth Star | Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | First White Water Star, Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star |- ! Sixth White Metal Star | First White Water Star, Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star | Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star |- ! Seventh Red Metal Star | First White Water Star, Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star | Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star |- ! Eighth White Earth Star | Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | First White Water Star, Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star |- ! Ninth Purple Fire Star | Second Black Earth Star, Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | Ninth Purple Fire Star | First White Water Star, Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star |}
These relationships are used for predicting {{ill|compatibility|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:相性]], {{ill|fortune|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:運勢]], and {{ill|auspicious directions|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:吉方位]].

{{ill|Bagua|ja|八卦}} '''Shin''' (震) is one of the {{ill|Bagua|ja|八卦}}. Its trigram form is {{resize|125%|'''☳'''}} with the first {{ill|Yao (lines)|ja|爻}} being Yang (⚊) and the second and third Yao being Yin (⚋). It is also one of the {{ill|Sixty-Four Hexagrams|ja|六十四卦}}, known as {{ill|Zhen as Thunder|ja|震為雷}}, consisting of Shin below and Shin above.
== Trigram Symbolism == The original meaning represents the emergence of a single Yang at the bottom, moving forward without limits. From this, it symbolizes the time of initiation, thunderclaps (the sound of {{ill|thunder|ja|雷}}), {{ill|dragons|ja|龍}}, {{ill|feet|ja|足}}, the eldest son, and young men. As a {{ill|direction|ja|方位}}, it represents {{ill|east|ja|東}}<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」National Diet Library, accessed December 16, 2021.</ref> (corresponding to {{ill|Mao (Earthly Branch)|ja|卯}} in the {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|地支}}). In modern society, it is associated with telecommunications, high-speed communication, and at times, deceit. Physically, it corresponds to hearing, the {{ill|liver|ja|肝臓}}, and {{ill|metabolism|ja|代謝}}.
In the {{ill|Na Jia|ja|納甲}} system, Shin is associated with {{ill|Geng (Heavenly Stem)|ja|庚}}, the Metal element in {{ill|Wu Xing|ja|五行思想}}, and the {{ill|west|ja|西}} among the {{ill|Five Directions|ja|五方}}.
== Earlier Heaven Arrangement == In the {{ill|Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|ja|伏羲先天八卦}}, Shin is the fourth in sequence and is positioned as one of the four corner trigrams, representing {{ill|northeast|ja|北東}}. In terms of Yin-Yang transformation, it represents the emergence of one Yang.

{{Bagua}} '''Shin''' (震) is one of the {{ill|Bagua|ja|八卦}}. Its trigram form is {{resize|125%|'''☳'''}} with the first {{ill|Yao (lines)|ja|爻}} being Yang (⚊) and the second and third Yao being Yin (⚋). It is also one of the {{ill|Sixty-Four Hexagrams|ja|六十四卦}}, known as {{ill|Zhen as Thunder|ja|震為雷}}, consisting of Shin below and Shin above.
== Trigram Symbolism == The original meaning represents the emergence of a single Yang at the bottom, moving forward without limits. From this, it symbolizes the time of initiation, thunderclaps (the sound of {{ill|thunder|ja|雷}}), {{ill|dragons|ja|龍}}, {{ill|feet|ja|足}}, the eldest son, and young men. As a {{ill|direction|ja|方位}}, it represents {{ill|east|ja|東}}<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」National Diet Library, accessed December 16, 2021.</ref> (corresponding to {{ill|Mao (Earthly Branch)|ja|卯}} in the {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|地支}}). In modern society, it is associated with telecommunications, high-speed communication, and at times, deceit. Physically, it corresponds to hearing, the {{ill|liver|ja|肝臓}}, and {{ill|metabolism|ja|代謝}}.
In the {{ill|Na Jia|ja|納甲}} system, Shin is associated with {{ill|Geng (Heavenly Stem)|ja|庚}}, the Metal element in {{ill|Wu Xing|ja|五行思想}}, and the {{ill|west|ja|西}} among the {{ill|Five Directions|ja|五方}}.
== Earlier Heaven Arrangement == In the {{ill|Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|ja|伏羲先天八卦}}, Shin is the fourth in sequence and is positioned as one of the four corner trigrams, representing {{ill|northeast|ja|北東}}. In terms of Yin-Yang transformation, it represents the emergence of one Yang.

{{Bagua}} '''Shin''' (震) is one of the {{ill|Bagua|ja|八卦}}. Its trigram form is {{resize|125%|'''☳'''}} with the first {{ill|Yao (lines)|ja|爻}} being Yang (⚊) and the second and third Yao being Yin (⚋). It is also one of the {{ill|Sixty-Four Hexagrams|ja|六十四卦}}, known as {{ill|Zhen as Thunder|ja|震為雷}}, consisting of Shin below and Shin above.
== Trigram Symbolism == The original meaning represents the emergence of a single Yang at the bottom, moving forward without limits. From this, it symbolizes the time of initiation, thunderclaps (the sound of {{ill|thunder|ja|雷}}), {{ill|dragons|ja|龍}}, {{ill|feet|ja|足}}, the eldest son, and young men. As a {{ill|direction|ja|方位}}, it represents {{ill|east|ja|東}}<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」National Diet Library, accessed December 16, 2021.</ref> (corresponding to {{ill|Mao (Earthly Branch)|ja|卯}} in the {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|地支}}). In modern society, it is associated with telecommunications, high-speed communication, and at times, deceit. Physically, it corresponds to hearing, the {{ill|liver|ja|肝臓}}, and {{ill|metabolism|ja|代謝}}.
In the {{ill|Na Jia|ja|納甲}} system, Shin is associated with {{ill|Geng (Heavenly Stem)|ja|庚}}, the Metal element in {{ill|Wuxing|lt=Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|ja|五行思想}}, and the {{ill|west|ja|西}} among the {{ill|Five Directions|ja|五方}}.
== Earlier Heaven Arrangement == In the {{ill|Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|ja|伏羲先天八卦}}, Shin is the fourth in sequence and is positioned as one of the four corner trigrams, representing {{ill|northeast|ja|北東}}. In terms of Yin-Yang transformation, it represents the emergence of one Yang.

{{Bagua}} '''Shin''' (震) is one of the {{ill|Bagua|ja|八卦}}. Its trigram form is {{resize|125%|'''☳'''}} with the first {{ill|Yao (lines)|ja|爻}} being Yang (⚊) and the second and third Yao being Yin (⚋). It is also one of the {{ill|Sixty-Four Hexagrams|ja|六十四卦}}, known as {{ill|Zhen as Thunder|ja|震為雷}}, consisting of Shin below and Shin above.
== Trigram Symbolism == The original meaning represents the emergence of a single Yang at the bottom, moving forward without limits. From this, it symbolizes the time of initiation, thunderclaps (the sound of {{ill|thunder|ja|雷}}), {{ill|dragons|ja|龍}}, {{ill|feet|ja|足}}, the eldest son, and young men. As a {{ill|cardinal direction|ja|方位}}, it represents {{ill|east|ja|東}}<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」National Diet Library, accessed December 16, 2021.</ref> (corresponding to {{ill|Mao (Earthly Branch)|ja|卯}} in the {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|地支}}). In modern society, it is associated with telecommunications, high-speed communication, and at times, deceit. Physically, it corresponds to hearing, the {{ill|liver|ja|肝臓}}, and {{ill|metabolism|ja|代謝}}.
In the {{ill|Na Jia|ja|納甲}} system, Shin is associated with {{ill|Geng (Heavenly Stem)|ja|庚}}, the Metal element in {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|lt=Wuxing|ja|五行思想}}, and the {{ill|west|ja|西}} among the {{ill|Five Directions|ja|五方}}.
== Earlier Heaven Arrangement == In the {{ill|Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|ja|伏羲先天八卦}}, Shin is the fourth in sequence and is positioned as one of the four corner trigrams, representing {{ill|northeast|ja|北東}}. In terms of Yin-Yang transformation, it represents the emergence of one Yang.

revision.comment: formatting see also and references and tables
revision.text:
{{Bagua}} '''Shin''' (震) is one of the {{ill|Bagua|ja|八卦}}. Its trigram form is {{resize|125%|'''☳'''}} with the first {{ill|Yao (lines)|ja|爻}} being Yang (⚊) and the second and third Yao being Yin (⚋). It is also one of the {{ill|Sixty-Four Hexagrams|ja|六十四卦}}, known as {{ill|Zhen as Thunder|ja|震為雷}}, consisting of Shin below and Shin above.
== Trigram Symbolism == The original meaning represents the emergence of a single Yang at the bottom, moving forward without limits. From this, it symbolizes the time of initiation, thunderclaps (the sound of {{ill|thunder|ja|雷}}), {{ill|dragons|ja|龍}}, {{ill|feet|ja|足}}, the eldest son, and young men. As a {{ill|cardinal direction|ja|方位}}, it represents {{ill|east|ja|東}}<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」National Diet Library, accessed December 16, 2021.</ref> (corresponding to {{ill|Mao (Earthly Branch)|ja|卯}} in the {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|地支}}). In modern society, it is associated with telecommunications, high-speed communication, and at times, deceit. Physically, it corresponds to hearing, the {{ill|liver|ja|肝臓}}, and {{ill|metabolism|ja|代謝}}.
In the {{ill|Na Jia|ja|納甲}} system, Shin is associated with {{ill|Geng (Heavenly Stem)|ja|庚}}, the Metal element in {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|lt=Wuxing|ja|五行思想}}, and the {{ill|west|ja|西}} among the {{ill|Five Directions|ja|五方}}.
== Earlier Heaven Arrangement == In the {{ill|Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|ja|伏羲先天八卦}}, Shin is the fourth in sequence and is positioned as one of the four corner trigrams, representing {{ill|northeast|ja|北東}}. In terms of Yin-Yang transformation, it represents the emergence of one Yang.
== Nine Stars Comparison Chart == {{main|{{ill|Nine Stars|ja|九星}}}}

[[nl:Donder (trigram)]] [[fr:Zh%C3%A8n]]

{{Bagua}} '''Shin''' (震) is one of the {{ill|Bagua|ja|八卦}}. Its trigram form is {{resize|125%|'''☳'''}} with the first {{ill|Yao (lines)|ja|爻}} being Yang (⚊) and the second and third Yao being Yin (⚋). It is also one of the {{ill|Sixty-Four Hexagrams|ja|六十四卦}}, known as {{ill|Zhen as Thunder|ja|震為雷}}, consisting of Shin below and Shin above.
== Trigram Symbolism == The original meaning represents the emergence of a single Yang at the bottom, moving forward without limits. From this, it symbolizes the time of initiation, thunderclaps (the sound of {{ill|thunder|ja|雷}}), {{ill|dragons|ja|龍}}, {{ill|feet|ja|足}}, the eldest son, and young men. As a {{ill|cardinal direction|ja|方位}}, it represents {{ill|east|ja|東}}<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」National Diet Library, accessed December 16, 2021.</ref> (corresponding to {{ill|Mao (Earthly Branch)|ja|卯}} in the {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|地支}}). In modern society, it is associated with telecommunications, high-speed communication, and at times, deceit. Physically, it corresponds to hearing, the {{ill|liver|ja|肝臓}}, and {{ill|metabolism|ja|代謝}}.
In the {{ill|Na Jia|ja|納甲}} system, Shin is associated with {{ill|Geng (Heavenly Stem)|ja|庚}}, the Metal element in {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|lt=Wuxing|ja|五行思想}}, and the {{ill|west|ja|西}} among the {{ill|Five Directions|ja|五方}}.
== Earlier Heaven Arrangement == In the {{ill|Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|ja|伏羲先天八卦}}, Shin is the fourth in sequence and is positioned as one of the four corner trigrams, representing {{ill|northeast|ja|北東}}. In terms of Yin-Yang transformation, it represents the emergence of one Yang.
== Nine Stars Comparison Chart == {{main|{{ill|Nine Stars|ja|九星}}}}

[[nl:Donder (trigram)]] [[fr:Zh%C3%A8n]]

{{Bagua}} '''Shin''' (震) is one of the {{ill|Bagua|ja|八卦}}. Its trigram form is {{resize|125%|'''☳'''}} with the first {{ill|Yao (lines)|ja|爻}} being Yang (⚊) and the second and third Yao being Yin (⚋). It is also one of the {{ill|Sixty-Four Hexagrams|ja|六十四卦}}, known as {{ill|Zhen as Thunder|ja|震為雷}}, consisting of Shin below and Shin above.
== Trigram Symbolism == The original meaning represents the emergence of a single Yang at the bottom, moving forward without limits. From this, it symbolizes the time of initiation, thunderclaps (the sound of {{ill|thunder|ja|雷}}), {{ill|dragons|ja|龍}}, {{ill|feet|ja|足}}, the eldest son, and young men. As a {{ill|cardinal direction|ja|方位}}, it represents {{ill|east|ja|東}}<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」National Diet Library, accessed December 16, 2021.</ref> (corresponding to {{ill|Mao (Earthly Branch)|ja|卯}} in the {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|地支}}). In modern society, it is associated with telecommunications, high-speed communication, and at times, deceit. Physically, it corresponds to hearing, the {{ill|liver|ja|肝臓}}, and {{ill|metabolism|ja|代謝}}.
In the {{ill|Na Jia|ja|納甲}} system, Shin is associated with {{ill|Geng (Heavenly Stem)|ja|庚}}, the Metal element in {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|lt=Wuxing|ja|五行思想}}, and the {{ill|west|ja|西}} among the {{ill|Five Directions|ja|五方}}.
== Earlier Heaven Arrangement == In the {{ill|Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|ja|伏羲先天八卦}}, Shin is the fourth in sequence and is positioned as one of the four corner trigrams, representing {{ill|northeast|ja|北東}}. In terms of Yin-Yang transformation, it represents the emergence of one Yang.

== Trigram Symbolism == The original meaning represents the emergence of a single Yang at the bottom, moving forward without limits. From this, it symbolizes the time of initiation, thunderclaps (the sound of {{ill|thunder|ja|雷}}), {{ill|dragons|ja|龍}}, {{ill|feet|ja|足}}, the eldest son, and young men. As a {{ill|cardinal direction|ja|方位}}, it represents {{ill|east|ja|東}}<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」National Diet Library, accessed December 16, 2021.</ref> (corresponding to {{ill|Mao (Earthly Branch)|ja|卯}} in the {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|地支}}). In modern society, it is associated with telecommunications, high-speed communication, and at times, deceit. Physically, it corresponds to hearing, the {{ill|liver|ja|肝臓}}, and {{ill|metabolism|ja|代謝}}.
In the {{ill|Na Jia|ja|納甲}} system, Shin is associated with {{ill|Geng (Heavenly Stem)|ja|庚}}, the Metal element in {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|lt=Wuxing|ja|五行思想}}, and the {{ill|west|ja|西}} among the {{ill|Five Directions|ja|五方}}.

revision.comment: attempting the mass replace that renames the trrrigrams
revision.text:
{{Bagua}} '''Zhen''' (震) is one of the {{ill|Bagua|ja|八卦}}. Its trigram form is {{resize|125%|'''☳'''}} with the first {{ill|Yao (lines)|ja|爻}} being Yang (⚊) and the second and third Yao being Yin (⚋). It is also one of the {{ill|Sixty-Four Hexagrams|ja|六十四卦}}, known as {{ill|Zhen as Thunder|ja|震|1=Zhen (Bagua)|lt=Zhen為雷}}, consisting of Shin below and Shin above.
== Trigram Symbolism == The original meaning represents the emergence of a single Yang at the bottom, moving forward without limits. From this, it symbolizes the time of initiation, thunderclaps (the sound of {{ill|thunder|ja|雷}}), {{ill|dragons|ja|龍}}, {{ill|feet|ja|足}}, the eldest son, and young men. As a {{ill|cardinal direction|ja|方位}}, it represents {{ill|east|ja|東}}<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」National Diet Library, accessed December 16, 2021.</ref> (corresponding to {{ill|Mao (Earthly Branch)|ja|卯}} in the {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|地支}}). In modern society, it is associated with telecommunications, high-speed communication, and at times, deceit. Physically, it corresponds to hearing, the {{ill|liver|ja|肝臓}}, and {{ill|metabolism|ja|代謝}}.
In the {{ill|Na Jia|ja|納甲}} system, Shin is associated with {{ill|Geng (Heavenly Stem)|ja|庚}}, the Metal element in {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|lt=Wuxing|ja|五行思想}}, and the {{ill|west|ja|西}} among the {{ill|Five Directions|ja|五方}}.
== Earlier Heaven Arrangement == In the {{ill|Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|ja|伏羲先天八卦}}, Shin is the fourth in sequence and is positioned as one of the four corner trigrams, representing {{ill|northeast|ja|北東}}. In terms of Yin-Yang transformation, it represents the emergence of one Yang.
== Nine Stars Comparison Chart == {{main|{{ill|Nine Stars|ja|九星}}}}

== Trigram Symbolism == The original meaning represents the emergence of a single Yang at the bottom, moving forward without limits. From this, it symbolizes the time of initiation, thunderclaps (the sound of {{ill|thunder|ja|雷}}), {{ill|dragons|ja|龍}}, {{ill|feet|ja|足}}, the eldest son, and young men. As a {{ill|cardinal direction|ja|方位}}, it represents {{ill|east|ja|東}}<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」National Diet Library, accessed December 16, 2021.</ref> (corresponding to {{ill|Mao (Earthly Branch)|ja|卯}} in the {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|地支}}). In modern society, it is associated with telecommunications, high-speed communication, and at times, deceit. Physically, it corresponds to hearing, the {{ill|liver|ja|肝臓}}, and {{ill|metabolism|ja|代謝}}.
In the {{ill|Na Jia|ja|納甲}} system, Shin is associated with {{ill|Geng (Heavenly Stem)|ja|庚}}, the Metal element in {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|lt=Wuxing|ja|五行思想}}, and the {{ill|west|ja|西}} among the {{ill|Five Directions|ja|五方}}.
== Earlier Heaven Arrangement == In the {{ill|Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|ja|伏羲先天八卦}}, Shin is the fourth in sequence and is positioned as one of the four corner trigrams, representing {{ill|northeast|ja|北東}}. In terms of Yin-Yang transformation, it represents the emergence of one Yang.

[[nl:Donder (trigram)]] [[fr:Zh%C3%A8n]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Zhen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Zhen (Bagua)]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[en:Zhen]]

{{Bagua}} '''Zhen''' (震) is one of the {{ill|Bagua|ja|八卦}}. Its trigram form is {{resize|125%|'''☳'''}} with the first {{ill|Yao (lines)|ja|爻}} being Yang (⚊) and the second and third Yao being Yin (⚋). It is also one of the {{ill|Sixty-Four Hexagrams|ja|六十四卦}}, known as {{ill|Zhen as Thunder|ja|震|1=Zhen (Bagua)|lt=Zhen為雷}}, consisting of Shin below and Shin above.
== Trigram Symbolism == The original meaning represents the emergence of a single Yang at the bottom, moving forward without limits. From this, it symbolizes the time of initiation, thunderclaps (the sound of {{ill|thunder|ja|雷}}), {{ill|dragons|ja|龍}}, {{ill|feet|ja|足}}, the eldest son, and young men. As a {{ill|cardinal direction|ja|方位}}, it represents {{ill|east|ja|東}}<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」National Diet Library, accessed December 16, 2021.</ref> (corresponding to {{ill|Mao (Earthly Branch)|ja|卯}} in the {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|地支}}). In modern society, it is associated with telecommunications, high-speed communication, and at times, deceit. Physically, it corresponds to hearing, the {{ill|liver|ja|肝臓}}, and {{ill|metabolism|ja|代謝}}.
In the {{ill|Na Jia|ja|納甲}} system, Shin is associated with {{ill|Geng (Heavenly Stem)|ja|庚}}, the Metal element in {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|lt=Wuxing|ja|五行思想}}, and the {{ill|west|ja|西}} among the {{ill|Five Directions|ja|五方}}.
== Earlier Heaven Arrangement == In the {{ill|Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|ja|伏羲先天八卦}}, Shin is the fourth in sequence and is positioned as one of the four corner trigrams, representing {{ill|northeast|ja|北東}}. In terms of Yin-Yang transformation, it represents the emergence of one Yang.

[[nl:Donder (trigram)]] [[fr:Zh%C3%A8n]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Zhen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Zhen (Bagua)]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[en:Zhen]]

{{Bagua}} '''Zhen''' (震) is one of the {{ill|Bagua|ja|八卦}}. Its trigram form is {{resize|125%|'''☳'''}} with the first {{ill|Yao (lines)|ja|爻}} being Yang (⚊) and the second and third Yao being Yin (⚋). It is also one of the {{ill|Sixty-Four Hexagrams|ja|六十四卦}}, known as {{ill|Zhen as Thunder|ja|震|1=Zhen (Bagua)|lt=Zhen為雷}}, consisting of Shin below and Shin above.
== Trigram Symbolism == The original meaning represents the emergence of a single Yang at the bottom, moving forward without limits. From this, it symbolizes the time of initiation, thunderclaps (the sound of {{ill|thunder|ja|雷}}), {{ill|dragons|ja|龍}}, {{ill|feet|ja|足}}, the eldest son, and young men. As a {{ill|cardinal direction|ja|方位}}, it represents {{ill|east|ja|東}}<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」National Diet Library, accessed December 16, 2021.</ref> (corresponding to {{ill|Mao (Earthly Branch)|ja|卯}} in the {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|地支}}). In modern society, it is associated with telecommunications, high-speed communication, and at times, deceit. Physically, it corresponds to hearing, the {{ill|liver|ja|肝臓}}, and {{ill|metabolism|ja|代謝}}.
In the {{ill|Na Jia|ja|納甲}} system, Shin is associated with {{ill|Geng (Heavenly Stem)|ja|庚}}, the Metal element in {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|lt=Wuxing|ja|五行思想}}, and the {{ill|west|ja|西}} among the {{ill|Five Directions|ja|五方}}.
== Earlier Heaven Arrangement == In the {{ill|Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|ja|伏羲先天八卦}}, Shin is the fourth in sequence and is positioned as one of the four corner trigrams, representing {{ill|northeast|ja|北東}}. In terms of Yin-Yang transformation, it represents the emergence of one Yang.

{{Bagua}} '''Zhen''' (震) is one of the {{ill|Bagua|ja|八卦}}. Its trigram form is {{resize|125%|'''☳'''}} with the first {{ill|Yao (lines)|ja|爻}} being Yang (⚊) and the second and third Yao being Yin (⚋). It is also one of the {{ill|Sixty-Four Hexagrams|ja|六十四卦}}, known as {{ill|Zhen as Thunder|ja|震|1=Zhen (Bagua)|lt=Zhen為雷}}, consisting of Shin below and Shin above.
== Trigram Symbolism == The original meaning represents the emergence of a single Yang at the bottom, moving forward without limits. From this, it symbolizes the time of initiation, thunderclaps (the sound of {{ill|thunder|ja|雷}}), {{ill|dragons|ja|龍}}, {{ill|feet|ja|足}}, the eldest son, and young men. As a {{ill|cardinal direction|ja|方位}}, it represents {{ill|east|ja|東}}<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」National Diet Library, accessed December 16, 2021.</ref> (corresponding to {{ill|Mao (Earthly Branch)|ja|卯}} in the {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|地支}}). In modern society, it is associated with telecommunications, high-speed communication, and at times, deceit. Physically, it corresponds to hearing, the {{ill|liver|ja|肝臓}}, and {{ill|metabolism|ja|代謝}}.
In the {{ill|Na Jia|ja|納甲}} system, Shin is associated with {{ill|Geng (Heavenly Stem)|ja|庚}}, the Metal element in {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|lt=Wuxing|ja|五行思想}}, and the {{ill|west|ja|西}} among the {{ill|Five Directions|ja|五方}}.
== Earlier Heaven Arrangement == In the {{ill|Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|ja|伏羲先天八卦}}, Shin is the fourth in sequence and is positioned as one of the four corner trigrams, representing {{ill|northeast|ja|北東}}. In terms of Yin-Yang transformation, it represents the emergence of one Yang.
== Nine Stars Comparison Chart == {{main|{{ill|Nine Stars|ja|九星}}}}

revision.text:
{{Bagua}} '''Zhen''' (震) is one of the {{ill|Bagua|ja|八卦}}. Its trigram form is {{resize|125%|'''☳'''}} with the first {{ill|Yao (lines)|ja|爻}} being Yang (⚊) and the second and third Yao being Yin (⚋). It is also one of the {{ill|Sixty-Four Hexagrams|ja|六十四卦}}, known as {{ill|Zhen as Thunder|ja|震|1=Zhen (Bagua)|lt=Zhen為雷}}, consisting of Shin below and Shin above.
== Trigram Symbolism == The original meaning represents the emergence of a single Yang at the bottom, moving forward without limits. From this, it symbolizes the time of initiation, thunderclaps (the sound of {{ill|thunder|ja|雷}}), {{ill|dragons|ja|龍}}, {{ill|feet|ja|足}}, the eldest son, and young men. As a {{ill|cardinal direction|ja|方位}}, it represents {{ill|east|ja|東}}<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」National Diet Library, accessed December 16, 2021.</ref> (corresponding to {{ill|Mao (Earthly Branch)|ja|卯}} in the {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|地支}}). In modern society, it is associated with telecommunications, high-speed communication, and at times, deceit. Physically, it corresponds to hearing, the {{ill|liver|ja|肝臓}}, and {{ill|metabolism|ja|代謝}}.
In the {{ill|Na Jia|ja|納甲}} system, Shin is associated with {{ill|Geng (Heavenly Stem)|ja|庚}}, the Metal element in {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|lt=Wuxing|ja|五行思想}}, and the {{ill|west|ja|西}} among the {{ill|Five Directions|ja|五方}}.
== Earlier Heaven Arrangement == In the {{ill|Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|ja|伏羲先天八卦}}, Shin is the fourth in sequence and is positioned as one of the four corner trigrams, representing {{ill|northeast|ja|北東}}. In terms of Yin-Yang transformation, it represents the emergence of one Yang.
== Nine Stars Comparison Chart == {{main|{{ill|Nine Stars|ja|九星}}}}
[[Category:I Ching]] [[ja:震]]

[[nl:Donder (trigram)]] [[fr:Zh%C3%A8n]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Zhen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Zhen (Bagua)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Zhen (Bagua)]][[en:Zhen]]

{{Bagua}} '''Zhen''' (震) is one of the {{ill|Bagua|ja|八卦}}. Its trigram form is {{resize|125%|'''☳'''}} with the first {{ill|Yao (lines)|ja|爻}} being Yang (⚊) and the second and third Yao being Yin (⚋). It is also one of the {{ill|Sixty-Four Hexagrams|ja|六十四卦}}, known as {{ill|Zhen as Thunder|ja|震|1=Zhen (Bagua)|lt=Zhen為雷}}, consisting of Shin below and Shin above.
== Trigram Symbolism == The original meaning represents the emergence of a single Yang at the bottom, moving forward without limits. From this, it symbolizes the time of initiation, thunderclaps (the sound of {{ill|thunder|ja|雷}}), {{ill|dragons|ja|龍}}, {{ill|feet|ja|足}}, the eldest son, and young men. As a {{ill|cardinal direction|ja|方位}}, it represents {{ill|east|ja|東}}<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」National Diet Library, accessed December 16, 2021.</ref> (corresponding to {{ill|Mao (Earthly Branch)|ja|卯}} in the {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|地支}}). In modern society, it is associated with telecommunications, high-speed communication, and at times, deceit. Physically, it corresponds to hearing, the {{ill|liver|ja|肝臓}}, and {{ill|metabolism|ja|代謝}}.
In the {{ill|Na Jia|ja|納甲}} system, Shin is associated with {{ill|Geng (Heavenly Stem)|ja|庚}}, the Metal element in {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|lt=Wuxing|ja|五行思想}}, and the {{ill|west|ja|西}} among the {{ill|Five Directions|ja|五方}}.
== Earlier Heaven Arrangement == In the {{ill|Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|ja|伏羲先天八卦}}, Shin is the fourth in sequence and is positioned as one of the four corner trigrams, representing {{ill|northeast|ja|北東}}. In terms of Yin-Yang transformation, it represents the emergence of one Yang.

[[nl:Donder (trigram)]] [[fr:Zh%C3%A8n]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Zhen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Zhen (Bagua)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Zhen (Bagua)]][[en:Zhen]]

{{Bagua}} '''Zhen''' (震) is one of the {{ill|Bagua|ja|八卦}}. Its trigram form is {{resize|125%|'''☳'''}} with the first {{ill|Yao (lines)|ja|爻}} being Yang (⚊) and the second and third Yao being Yin (⚋). It is also one of the {{ill|Sixty-Four Hexagrams|ja|六十四卦}}, known as {{ill|Zhen as Thunder|ja|震|1=Zhen (Bagua)|lt=Zhen為雷}}, consisting of Shin below and Shin above.
== Trigram Symbolism == The original meaning represents the emergence of a single Yang at the bottom, moving forward without limits. From this, it symbolizes the time of initiation, thunderclaps (the sound of {{ill|thunder|ja|雷}}), {{ill|dragons|ja|龍}}, {{ill|feet|ja|足}}, the eldest son, and young men. As a {{ill|cardinal direction|ja|方位}}, it represents {{ill|east|ja|東}}<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」National Diet Library, accessed December 16, 2021.</ref> (corresponding to {{ill|Mao (Earthly Branch)|ja|卯}} in the {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|地支}}). In modern society, it is associated with telecommunications, high-speed communication, and at times, deceit. Physically, it corresponds to hearing, the {{ill|liver|ja|肝臓}}, and {{ill|metabolism|ja|代謝}}.
In the {{ill|Na Jia|ja|納甲}} system, Shin is associated with {{ill|Geng (Heavenly Stem)|ja|庚|1=Kanoe (庚)}}, the Metal element in {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|lt=Wuxing|ja|五行思想}}, and the {{ill|west|ja|西}} among the {{ill|Five Directions|ja|五方}}.
== Earlier Heaven Arrangement == In the {{ill|Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|ja|伏羲先天八卦}}, Shin is the fourth in sequence and is positioned as one of the four corner trigrams, representing {{ill|northeast|ja|北東}}. In terms of Yin-Yang transformation, it represents the emergence of one Yang.

== Trigram Symbolism == The original meaning represents the emergence of a single Yang at the bottom, moving forward without limits. From this, it symbolizes the time of initiation, thunderclaps (the sound of {{ill|thunder|ja|雷}}), {{ill|dragons|ja|龍}}, {{ill|feet|ja|足}}, the eldest son, and young men. As a {{ill|cardinal direction|ja|方位}}, it represents {{ill|east|ja|東}}<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」National Diet Library, accessed December 16, 2021.</ref> (corresponding to {{ill|Mao (Earthly Branch)|ja|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)}} in the {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|地支}}). In modern society, it is associated with telecommunications, high-speed communication, and at times, deceit. Physically, it corresponds to hearing, the {{ill|liver|ja|肝臓}}, and {{ill|metabolism|ja|代謝}}.
In the {{ill|Na Jia|ja|納甲}} system, Zhen is associated with {{ill|Geng (Heavenly Stem)|ja|庚|1=Kanoe (庚)}}, the Metal element in {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|lt=Wuxing|ja|五行思想}}, and the {{ill|west|ja|西}} among the {{ill|Five Directions|ja|五方}}.
== Earlier Heaven Arrangement == In the {{ill|Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|ja|伏羲先天八卦}}, Zhen is the fourth in sequence and is positioned as one of the four corner trigrams, representing {{ill|northeast|ja|北東}}. In terms of Yin-Yang transformation, it represents the emergence of one Yang.

{{Bagua}} '''Zhen''' (震) is one of the {{ill|Bagua|ja|八卦}}. Its trigram form is {{resize|125%|'''☳'''}} with the first {{ill|Yao (lines)|ja|爻}} being Yang (⚊) and the second and third Yao being Yin (⚋). It is also one of the {{ill|Sixty-Four Hexagrams|ja|六十四卦}}, known as {{ill|Zhen as Thunder|ja|震|1=Zhen (Bagua)|lt=Zhen為雷}}, consisting of Zhen below and Zhen above.
== Trigram Symbolism == The original meaning represents the emergence of a single Yang at the bottom, moving forward without limits. From this, it symbolizes the time of initiation, thunderclaps (the sound of {{ill|thunder|ja|雷}}), {{ill|dragons|ja|龍}}, {{ill|feet|ja|足}}, the eldest son, and young men. As a {{ill|cardinal direction|ja|方位}}, it represents {{ill|east|ja|東}}<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」National Diet Library, accessed December 16, 2021.</ref> (corresponding to {{ill|Mao (Earthly Branch)|ja|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)}} in the {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|地支}}). In modern society, it is associated with telecommunications, high-speed communication, and at times, deceit. Physically, it corresponds to hearing, the {{ill|liver|ja|肝臓}}, and {{ill|metabolism|ja|代謝}}.
In the {{ill|Na Jia|ja|納甲}} system, Zhen is associated with {{ill|Geng (Heavenly Stem)|ja|庚|1=Kanoe (庚)}}, the Metal element in {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|lt=Wuxing|ja|五行思想}}, and the {{ill|west|ja|西}} among the {{ill|Five Directions|ja|五方}}.
== Earlier Heaven Arrangement == In the {{ill|Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|ja|伏羲先天八卦}}, Zhen is the fourth in sequence and is positioned as one of the four corner trigrams, representing {{ill|northeast|ja|北東}}. In terms of Yin-Yang transformation, it represents the emergence of one Yang.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|一白水星}}" to "|一白水星|1=First White Water Star}}"
revision.text:
{{Bagua}} '''Zhen''' (震) is one of the {{ill|Bagua|ja|八卦}}. Its trigram form is {{resize|125%|'''☳'''}} with the first {{ill|Yao (lines)|ja|爻}} being Yang (⚊) and the second and third Yao being Yin (⚋). It is also one of the {{ill|Sixty-Four Hexagrams|ja|六十四卦}}, known as {{ill|Zhen as Thunder|ja|震|1=Zhen (Bagua)|lt=Zhen為雷}}, consisting of Zhen below and Zhen above.
== Trigram Symbolism == The original meaning represents the emergence of a single Yang at the bottom, moving forward without limits. From this, it symbolizes the time of initiation, thunderclaps (the sound of {{ill|thunder|ja|雷}}), {{ill|dragons|ja|龍}}, {{ill|feet|ja|足}}, the eldest son, and young men. As a {{ill|cardinal direction|ja|方位}}, it represents {{ill|east|ja|東}}<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」National Diet Library, accessed December 16, 2021.</ref> (corresponding to {{ill|Mao (Earthly Branch)|ja|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)}} in the {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|地支}}). In modern society, it is associated with telecommunications, high-speed communication, and at times, deceit. Physically, it corresponds to hearing, the {{ill|liver|ja|肝臓}}, and {{ill|metabolism|ja|代謝}}.
In the {{ill|Na Jia|ja|納甲}} system, Zhen is associated with {{ill|Geng (Heavenly Stem)|ja|庚|1=Kanoe (庚)}}, the Metal element in {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|lt=Wuxing|ja|五行思想}}, and the {{ill|west|ja|西}} among the {{ill|Five Directions|ja|五方}}.
== Earlier Heaven Arrangement == In the {{ill|Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|ja|伏羲先天八卦}}, Zhen is the fourth in sequence and is positioned as one of the four corner trigrams, representing {{ill|northeast|ja|北東}}. In terms of Yin-Yang transformation, it represents the emergence of one Yang.
== Nine Stars Comparison Chart == {{main|{{ill|Nine Stars|ja|九星}}}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|ja|四緑木星}}" to "|ja|四緑木星|1=Fourth Green Wood Star}}"
revision.text:
{{Bagua}} '''Zhen''' (震) is one of the {{ill|Bagua|ja|八卦}}. Its trigram form is {{resize|125%|'''☳'''}} with the first {{ill|Yao (lines)|ja|爻}} being Yang (⚊) and the second and third Yao being Yin (⚋). It is also one of the {{ill|Sixty-Four Hexagrams|ja|六十四卦}}, known as {{ill|Zhen as Thunder|ja|震|1=Zhen (Bagua)|lt=Zhen為雷}}, consisting of Zhen below and Zhen above.
== Trigram Symbolism == The original meaning represents the emergence of a single Yang at the bottom, moving forward without limits. From this, it symbolizes the time of initiation, thunderclaps (the sound of {{ill|thunder|ja|雷}}), {{ill|dragons|ja|龍}}, {{ill|feet|ja|足}}, the eldest son, and young men. As a {{ill|cardinal direction|ja|方位}}, it represents {{ill|east|ja|東}}<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」National Diet Library, accessed December 16, 2021.</ref> (corresponding to {{ill|Mao (Earthly Branch)|ja|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)}} in the {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|地支}}). In modern society, it is associated with telecommunications, high-speed communication, and at times, deceit. Physically, it corresponds to hearing, the {{ill|liver|ja|肝臓}}, and {{ill|metabolism|ja|代謝}}.
In the {{ill|Na Jia|ja|納甲}} system, Zhen is associated with {{ill|Geng (Heavenly Stem)|ja|庚|1=Kanoe (庚)}}, the Metal element in {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|lt=Wuxing|ja|五行思想}}, and the {{ill|west|ja|西}} among the {{ill|Five Directions|ja|五方}}.
== Earlier Heaven Arrangement == In the {{ill|Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|ja|伏羲先天八卦}}, Zhen is the fourth in sequence and is positioned as one of the four corner trigrams, representing {{ill|northeast|ja|北東}}. In terms of Yin-Yang transformation, it represents the emergence of one Yang.
== Nine Stars Comparison Chart == {{main|{{ill|Nine Stars|ja|九星}}}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|ja|七赤金星}}" to "|1=Seventh Red Metal Star|ja|七赤金星}}"
revision.text:
{{Bagua}} '''Zhen''' (震) is one of the {{ill|Bagua|ja|八卦}}. Its trigram form is {{resize|125%|'''☳'''}} with the first {{ill|Yao (lines)|ja|爻}} being Yang (⚊) and the second and third Yao being Yin (⚋). It is also one of the {{ill|Sixty-Four Hexagrams|ja|六十四卦}}, known as {{ill|Zhen as Thunder|ja|震|1=Zhen (Bagua)|lt=Zhen為雷}}, consisting of Zhen below and Zhen above.
== Trigram Symbolism == The original meaning represents the emergence of a single Yang at the bottom, moving forward without limits. From this, it symbolizes the time of initiation, thunderclaps (the sound of {{ill|thunder|ja|雷}}), {{ill|dragons|ja|龍}}, {{ill|feet|ja|足}}, the eldest son, and young men. As a {{ill|cardinal direction|ja|方位}}, it represents {{ill|east|ja|東}}<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」National Diet Library, accessed December 16, 2021.</ref> (corresponding to {{ill|Mao (Earthly Branch)|ja|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)}} in the {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|地支}}). In modern society, it is associated with telecommunications, high-speed communication, and at times, deceit. Physically, it corresponds to hearing, the {{ill|liver|ja|肝臓}}, and {{ill|metabolism|ja|代謝}}.
In the {{ill|Na Jia|ja|納甲}} system, Zhen is associated with {{ill|Geng (Heavenly Stem)|ja|庚|1=Kanoe (庚)}}, the Metal element in {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|lt=Wuxing|ja|五行思想}}, and the {{ill|west|ja|西}} among the {{ill|Five Directions|ja|五方}}.
== Earlier Heaven Arrangement == In the {{ill|Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|ja|伏羲先天八卦}}, Zhen is the fourth in sequence and is positioned as one of the four corner trigrams, representing {{ill|northeast|ja|北東}}. In terms of Yin-Yang transformation, it represents the emergence of one Yang.
== Nine Stars Comparison Chart == {{main|{{ill|Nine Stars|ja|九星}}}}

{{Bagua}} '''Zhen''' (震) is one of the {{ill|Bagua|ja|八卦}}. Its trigram form is {{resize|125%|'''☳'''}} with the first {{ill|Yao (lines)|ja|爻}} being Yang (⚊) and the second and third Yao being Yin (⚋). It is also one of the {{ill|Sixty-Four Hexagrams|ja|六十四卦}}, known as {{ill|Zhen as Thunder|ja|震|1=Zhen (Bagua)|lt=Zhen為雷}}, consisting of Zhen below and Zhen above.
== Trigram Symbolism == The original meaning represents the emergence of a single Yang at the bottom, moving forward without limits. From this, it symbolizes the time of initiation, thunderclaps (the sound of {{ill|thunder|ja|雷}}), {{ill|dragons|ja|龍}}, {{ill|feet|ja|足}}, the eldest son, and young men. As a {{ill|cardinal direction|ja|方位}}, it represents {{ill|east|ja|東}}<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」National Diet Library, accessed December 16, 2021.</ref> (corresponding to {{ill|Mao (Earthly Branch)|ja|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)}} in the {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|地支}}). In modern society, it is associated with telecommunications, high-speed communication, and at times, deceit. Physically, it corresponds to hearing, the {{ill|liver|ja|肝臓}}, and {{ill|metabolism|ja|代謝}}.
In the {{ill|Na Jia|ja|納甲}} system, Zhen is associated with {{ill|Geng (Heavenly Stem)|ja|庚|1=Kanoe (庚)}}, the Metal element in {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|lt=Wuxing|ja|五行思想}}, and the {{ill|west|ja|西}} among the {{ill|Five Directions|ja|五方}}.
== Earlier Heaven Arrangement == In the {{ill|Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|ja|伏羲先天八卦}}, Zhen is the fourth in sequence and is positioned as one of the four corner trigrams, representing {{ill|northeast|ja|北東}}. In terms of Yin-Yang transformation, it represents the emergence of one Yang.

{{Bagua}} '''Zhen''' (震) is one of the {{ill|Bagua|ja|八卦}}. Its trigram form is {{resize|125%|'''☳'''}} with the first {{ill|Yao (lines)|ja|爻}} being Yang (⚊) and the second and third Yao being Yin (⚋). It is also one of the {{ill|Sixty-Four Hexagrams|ja|六十四卦}}, known as {{ill|Zhen as Thunder|ja|震|1=Zhen (Bagua)|lt=Zhen為雷}}, consisting of Zhen below and Zhen above.
== Trigram Symbolism == The original meaning represents the emergence of a single Yang at the bottom, moving forward without limits. From this, it symbolizes the time of initiation, thunderclaps (the sound of {{ill|thunder|ja|雷}}), {{ill|dragons|ja|龍}}, {{ill|feet|ja|足}}, the eldest son, and young men. As a {{ill|cardinal direction|ja|方位}}, it represents {{ill|east|ja|東}}<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」National Diet Library, accessed December 16, 2021.</ref> (corresponding to {{ill|Mao (Earthly Branch)|ja|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)}} in the {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|地支}}). In modern society, it is associated with telecommunications, high-speed communication, and at times, deceit. Physically, it corresponds to hearing, the {{ill|liver|ja|肝臓}}, and {{ill|metabolism|ja|代謝}}.
In the {{ill|Na Jia|ja|納甲}} system, Zhen is associated with {{ill|Geng (Heavenly Stem)|ja|庚|1=Kanoe (庚)}}, the Metal element in {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|lt=Wuxing|ja|五行思想}}, and the {{ill|west|ja|西}} among the {{ill|Five Directions|ja|五方}}.

revision.text:
{{Bagua}} '''Zhen''' (震) is one of the {{ill|Bagua|ja|八卦}}. Its trigram form is {{resize|125%|'''☳'''}} with the first {{ill|Yao (lines)|ja|爻}} being Yang (⚊) and the second and third Yao being Yin (⚋). It is also one of the {{ill|Sixty-Four Hexagrams|ja|六十四卦}}, known as {{ill|Zhen as Thunder|ja|震|1=Zhen (Bagua)|lt=Zhen為雷}}, consisting of Zhen below and Zhen above.
== Trigram Symbolism == The original meaning represents the emergence of a single Yang at the bottom, moving forward without limits. From this, it symbolizes the time of initiation, thunderclaps (the sound of {{ill|thunder|ja|雷}}), {{ill|dragons|ja|龍}}, {{ill|feet|ja|足}}, the eldest son, and young men. As a {{ill|cardinal direction|ja|方位}}, it represents {{ill|east|ja|東}}<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」National Diet Library, accessed December 16, 2021.</ref> (corresponding to {{ill|Mao (Earthly Branch)|ja|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)}} in the {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|地支}}). In modern society, it is associated with telecommunications, high-speed communication, and at times, deceit. Physically, it corresponds to hearing, the {{ill|liver|ja|肝臓}}, and {{ill|metabolism|ja|代謝}}.
In the {{ill|Na Jia|ja|納甲}} system, Zhen is associated with {{ill|Geng (Heavenly Stem)|ja|庚|1=Kanoe (庚)}}, the Metal element in {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|lt=Wuxing|ja|五行思想}}, and the {{ill|west|ja|西}} among the {{ill|Five Directions|ja|五方}}.
== Earlier Heaven Arrangement == In the {{ill|Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|ja|伏羲先天八卦}}, Zhen is the fourth in sequence and is positioned as one of the four corner trigrams, representing {{ill|northeast|ja|北東}}. In terms of Yin-Yang transformation, it represents the emergence of one Yang.
== Nine Stars Comparison Chart == {{main|{{ill|Nine Stars|ja|九星}}}}

{{Bagua}} '''Zhen''' (震) is one of the {{ill|Bagua|ja|八卦|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its trigram form is {{resize|125%|'''☳'''}} with the first {{ill|Yao (lines)|ja|爻|12=draft|12=draft}} being Yang (⚊) and the second and third Yao being Yin (⚋). It is also one of the {{ill|Sixty-Four Hexagrams|ja|六十四卦|12=draft|12=draft}}, known as {{ill|Zhen as Thunder|ja|震|1=Zhen (Bagua)|lt=Zhen為雷|12=draft|12=draft}}, consisting of Zhen below and Zhen above.
== Trigram Symbolism == The original meaning represents the emergence of a single Yang at the bottom, moving forward without limits. From this, it symbolizes the time of initiation, thunderclaps (the sound of {{ill|thunder|ja|雷|12=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|dragons|ja|龍|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|feet|ja|足|12=draft|12=draft}}, the eldest son, and young men. As a {{ill|cardinal direction|ja|方位|12=draft|12=draft}}, it represents {{ill|east|ja|東|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」National Diet Library, accessed December 16, 2021.</ref> (corresponding to {{ill|Mao (Earthly Branch)|ja|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|12=draft|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|地支|12=draft|12=draft}}). In modern society, it is associated with telecommunications, high-speed communication, and at times, deceit. Physically, it corresponds to hearing, the {{ill|liver|ja|肝臓|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|metabolism|ja|代謝|12=draft|12=draft}}.
In the {{ill|Na Jia|ja|納甲|12=draft|12=draft}} system, Zhen is associated with {{ill|Geng (Heavenly Stem)|ja|庚|1=Kanoe (庚)|12=draft|12=draft}}, the Metal element in {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|lt=Wuxing|ja|五行思想|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|west|ja|西|12=draft|12=draft}} among the {{ill|Five Directions|ja|五方|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Earlier Heaven Arrangement == In the {{ill|Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|ja|伏羲先天八卦|12=draft|12=draft}}, Zhen is the fourth in sequence and is positioned as one of the four corner trigrams, representing {{ill|northeast|ja|北東|12=draft|12=draft}}. In terms of Yin-Yang transformation, it represents the emergence of one Yang.
== Nine Stars Comparison Chart == {{main|{{ill|Nine Stars|ja|九星|12=draft|12=draft}}}}

revision.text:
{{八卦}} '''震'''（しん）は[[八卦]]の一つ。卦の形は{{resize|125%|'''☳'''}}であり、初[[爻]]は[[陽]]、第2爻・第3爻は陰で構成される。または[[六十四卦]]の一つであり、[[周易下経三十四卦の一覧#震|震為雷]]。震下震上で構成される。
== 卦象 == 原義は最も下(創め)に一陽が生じて、限りなく前進する様子。ここから始動の時・雷鳴（[[雷]]の音）・[[龍]]・[[足]]・長男・若い男性などを象徴する。[[方位]]としては東を示す<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」国立国会図書館 2021年12月16日閲覧</ref>（[[地支]]では[[卯]]と一致）。現代社会では電信電話・高速通信など。時に嘘つきにもなる。肉体では聴覚・[[肝臓]]・[[代謝]]など。
[[納甲]]では[[庚]]、[[五行思想|五行]]の[[金]]、[[五方]]の[[西]]が当てられる。
== 先天図 == 伏羲先天八卦における次序は四であり、方位は四隅卦の一つで[[北東]]に配される。陰陽消息は一陽が生じたところである。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:しん}} [[Category:易]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[震]] to [[Zhen (Bagua)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q2650821]] Wikidata name: zhèn [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Bagua|qq=}} '''Zhen''' (震) is one of the [[:en:Bagua|Bagua]]. Its trigram form is {{resize|125%|'''☳'''|qq=}} with the first {{ill|Yao (lines)|ja|爻|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yao (lines)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yao (lines)|qq=}} being Yang (⚊) and the second and third Yao being Yin (⚋). It is also one of the [[:en:Hexagram (I Ching)|Sixty-Four Hexagrams]], known as {{ill|Zhen as Thunder|ja|震|1=Zhèn (Bagua)|2=ja|3=震|lt=Zhèn (震)|1=Zhèn (Bagua)|lt=Zhen為雷|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhen as Thunder|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhèn (Bagua)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhen as Thunder|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhèn (Bagua)|qq=}}, consisting of Zhen below and Zhen above.
== Trigram Symbolism == The original meaning represents the emergence of a single Yang at the bottom, moving forward without limits. From this, it symbolizes the time of initiation, thunderclaps (the sound of [[:en:Lightning|thunder]]), {{ill|dragons|ja|龍|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dragons|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dragons|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竜}}, [[:en:Foot|feet]], the eldest son, and young men. As a [[:en:Cardinal direction|Five directions]], it represents [[:en:East|east]]<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」National Diet Library, accessed December 16, 2021.</ref> (corresponding to {{ill|Mao (Earthly Branch)|ja|卯|1=Rabbit (Zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mao (Earthly Branch)|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mao (Earthly Branch)|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (Zodiac)|qq=}} in the {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|地支|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earthly Branches|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earthly Branches|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 十二支}}). In modern society, it is associated with telecommunications, high-speed communication, and at times, deceit. Physically, it corresponds to hearing, the [[:en:Liver|liver]], and [[:en:Metabolism|metabolism]].
In the {{ill|Na Jia|ja|納甲|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Na Jia|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Na Jia|qq=}} system, Zhen is associated with {{ill|Geng (Heavenly Stem)|ja|庚|1=Kanoe (庚)|lt=Kanoe (庚)|2=ja|3=庚|4=zh|5=庚|1=Kanoe (庚)|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geng (Heavenly Stem)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (庚)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geng (Heavenly Stem)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (庚)|qq=}}, the Metal element in [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Elements (Wuxing)]], and the [[:en:West|west]] among the [[:en:Cardinal direction|Five Directions]].
== Earlier Heaven Arrangement == In the {{ill|Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|ja|伏羲先天八卦|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Zhen is the fourth in sequence and is positioned as one of the four corner trigrams, representing {{ill|northeast|ja|北東|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/northeast|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/northeast|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. In terms of Yin-Yang transformation, it represents the emergence of one Yang.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q2650821]] Wikidata name: zhèn [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Bagua|qq=}} '''Zhen''' (震) is one of the [[:en:Bagua|Bagua]]. Its trigram form is {{resize|125%|'''☳'''|qq=}} with the first {{ill|Yao (lines)|ja|爻|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yao (lines)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yao (lines)|qq=}} being Yang (⚊) and the second and third Yao being Yin (⚋). It is also one of the [[:en:Hexagram (I Ching)|Sixty-Four Hexagrams]], known as {{ill|Zhen as Thunder|ja|震|1=Zhèn (Bagua)|2=ja|3=震|lt=Zhèn (震)|1=Zhèn (Bagua)|lt=Zhen為雷|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhen as Thunder|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhèn (Bagua)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhen as Thunder|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhèn (Bagua)|qq=}}, consisting of Zhen below and Zhen above.
== Trigram Symbolism == The original meaning represents the emergence of a single Yang at the bottom, moving forward without limits. From this, it symbolizes the time of initiation, thunderclaps (the sound of [[:en:Lightning|thunder]]), {{ill|dragons|ja|龍|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dragons|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dragons|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竜}}, [[:en:Foot|feet]], the eldest son, and young men. As a [[:en:Cardinal direction|Five directions]], it represents [[:en:East|east]]<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」National Diet Library, accessed December 16, 2021.</ref> (corresponding to {{ill|Mao (Earthly Branch)|ja|卯|1=Rabbit (Zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mao (Earthly Branch)|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mao (Earthly Branch)|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (Zodiac)|qq=}} in the {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|地支|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earthly Branches|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earthly Branches|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 十二支}}). In modern society, it is associated with telecommunications, high-speed communication, and at times, deceit. Physically, it corresponds to hearing, the [[:en:Liver|liver]], and [[:en:Metabolism|metabolism]].
In the {{ill|Na Jia|ja|納甲|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Na Jia|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Na Jia|qq=}} system, Zhen is associated with {{ill|Geng (Heavenly Stem)|ja|庚|1=Kanoe (庚)|lt=Kanoe (庚)|2=ja|3=庚|4=zh|5=庚|1=Kanoe (庚)|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geng (Heavenly Stem)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (庚)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geng (Heavenly Stem)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (庚)|qq=}}, the Metal element in [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Elements (Wuxing)]], and the [[:en:West|west]] among the [[:en:Cardinal direction|Five Directions]].
== Earlier Heaven Arrangement == In the {{ill|Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|ja|伏羲先天八卦|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Zhen is the fourth in sequence and is positioned as one of the four corner trigrams, representing {{ill|northeast|ja|北東|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/northeast|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/northeast|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. In terms of Yin-Yang transformation, it represents the emergence of one Yang.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|[[:en:Taiji (philosophy)|Taiji (太極)]]↵|[[:en:Taiji (philosophy)|Taiji]]" to "| colspan="2" |[[:en:Taiji (philosophy)|Taiji (太極)]]"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q2650821]] Wikidata name: zhèn [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Bagua|qq=}} '''Zhen''' (震) is one of the [[:en:Bagua|Bagua]]. Its trigram form is {{resize|125%|'''☳'''|qq=}} with the first {{ill|Yao (lines)|ja|爻|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yao (lines)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yao (lines)|qq=}} being Yang (⚊) and the second and third Yao being Yin (⚋). It is also one of the [[:en:Hexagram (I Ching)|Sixty-Four Hexagrams]], known as {{ill|Zhen as Thunder|ja|震|1=Zhèn (Bagua)|2=ja|3=震|lt=Zhèn (震)|1=Zhèn (Bagua)|lt=Zhen為雷|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhen as Thunder|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhèn (Bagua)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhen as Thunder|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhèn (Bagua)|qq=}}, consisting of Zhen below and Zhen above.
== Trigram Symbolism == The original meaning represents the emergence of a single Yang at the bottom, moving forward without limits. From this, it symbolizes the time of initiation, thunderclaps (the sound of [[:en:Lightning|thunder]]), {{ill|dragons|ja|龍|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dragons|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dragons|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竜}}, [[:en:Foot|feet]], the eldest son, and young men. As a [[:en:Cardinal direction|Five directions]], it represents [[:en:East|east]]<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」National Diet Library, accessed December 16, 2021.</ref> (corresponding to {{ill|Mao (Earthly Branch)|ja|卯|1=Rabbit (Zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mao (Earthly Branch)|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mao (Earthly Branch)|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (Zodiac)|qq=}} in the {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|地支|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earthly Branches|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earthly Branches|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 十二支}}). In modern society, it is associated with telecommunications, high-speed communication, and at times, deceit. Physically, it corresponds to hearing, the [[:en:Liver|liver]], and [[:en:Metabolism|metabolism]].
In the {{ill|Na Jia|ja|納甲|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Na Jia|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Na Jia|qq=}} system, Zhen is associated with {{ill|Geng (Heavenly Stem)|ja|庚|1=Kanoe (庚)|lt=Kanoe (庚)|2=ja|3=庚|4=zh|5=庚|1=Kanoe (庚)|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geng (Heavenly Stem)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (庚)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geng (Heavenly Stem)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (庚)|qq=}}, the Metal element in [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Elements (Wuxing)]], and the [[:en:West|west]] among the [[:en:Cardinal direction|Five Directions]].
== Earlier Heaven Arrangement == In the {{ill|Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|ja|伏羲先天八卦|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Zhen is the fourth in sequence and is positioned as one of the four corner trigrams, representing {{ill|northeast|ja|北東|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/northeast|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/northeast|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. In terms of Yin-Yang transformation, it represents the emergence of one Yang.
== Nine Stars Comparison Chart == {{main|{{ill|Nine Stars|ja|九星|1=Nine Stars|2=ja|3=九星|lt=Nine Stars|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Stars|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Stars|qq=}}|qq=}}

Star|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First White Water Star|13=User:Immanuelle/First White Water Star|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First White Water Star|13=User:Immanuelle/First White {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q2650821]] Wikidata name: zhèn [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Bagua|qq=}} '''Zhen''' (震) is one of the [[:en:Bagua|Bagua]]. Its trigram form is {{resize|125%|'''☳'''|qq=}} with the first {{ill|Yao (lines)|ja|爻|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yao (lines)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yao (lines)|qq=}} being Yang (⚊) and the second and third Yao being Yin (⚋). It is also one of the [[:en:Hexagram (I Ching)|Sixty-Four Hexagrams]], known as {{ill|Zhen as Thunder|ja|震|1=Zhèn (Bagua)|2=ja|3=震|lt=Zhèn (震)|1=Zhèn (Bagua)|lt=Zhen為雷|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhen as Thunder|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhèn (Bagua)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhen as Thunder|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhèn (Bagua)|qq=}}, consisting of Zhen below and Zhen above.
== Trigram Symbolism == The original meaning represents the emergence of a single Yang at the bottom, moving forward without limits. From this, it symbolizes the time of initiation, thunderclaps (the sound of [[:en:Lightning|thunder]]), {{ill|dragons|ja|龍|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dragons|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dragons|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竜|1=dragon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7559}}, [[:en:Foot|feet]], the eldest son, and young men. As a [[:en:Cardinal direction|Five directions]], it represents [[:en:East|east]]<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」National Diet Library, accessed December 16, 2021.</ref> (corresponding to {{ill|Mao (Earthly Branch)|ja|卯|1=mǎo|lt=Rabbit (卯)|2=ja|3=卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mao (Earthly Branch)|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mao (Earthly Branch)|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (Zodiac)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16662792}} in the {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|地支|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earthly Branches|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earthly Branches|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 十二支|1=Earthly Branches|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1072961}}). In modern society, it is associated with telecommunications, high-speed communication, and at times, deceit. Physically, it corresponds to hearing, the [[:en:Liver|liver]], and [[:en:Metabolism|metabolism]].
In the {{ill|Na Jia|ja|納甲|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Na Jia|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Na Jia|qq=}} system, Zhen is associated with {{ill|Geng (Heavenly Stem)|ja|庚|1=Kanoe (庚)|lt=Kanoe (庚)|2=ja|3=庚|4=zh|5=庚|1=Kanoe (庚)|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geng (Heavenly Stem)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (庚)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geng (Heavenly Stem)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (庚)|qq=}}, the Metal element in [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Elements (Wuxing)]], and the [[:en:West|west]] among the [[:en:Cardinal direction|Five Directions]].
== Earlier Heaven Arrangement == In the {{ill|Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|ja|伏羲先天八卦|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Zhen is the fourth in sequence and is positioned as one of the four corner trigrams, representing {{ill|northeast|ja|北東|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/northeast|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/northeast|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=northeast|WD=Q6497686}}. In terms of Yin-Yang transformation, it represents the emergence of one Yang.
